sn9.4:1.1: On one occasion many monks were staying among the Kosalans in a forest grove.
sn9.4:1.2: Then, at the end of the Rains, they left to go on a three-month wandering tour.
sn9.4:1.3: And at that time, the devatā living in that forest grove, lamenting from not seeing those monks, recited this verse:
sn9.4:2.1: “Today it’s as if discontent is displayed to me,
sn9.4:2.2: as I see many empty seats.
sn9.4:2.4: Where have those disciples of Gotama gone?”
sn9.4:3.1: When this was said, another devatā recited a verse for that devatā:
sn9.4:4.1: “They’ve gone to Magadha, gone to Kosala,
sn9.4:4.2: & some to the land of the Vajjians.
sn9.4:4.3: Like deer, 	wandering free from ties,
